plain_text_links_link_gate.label: Hiring in the United States, overview

compliance_cache_used: sprint/compliance-cache/georgia.json (FMLA / PWFA / federal-floor data layered on top of US federal cache; Georgia-specific Family Care Act and HB 1010 verified from primary OCGA and legis.ga.gov sources, not present in cache)
high_concern_flags: factual_uncertainty: Georgia Family Care Act (OCGA § 34-1-10) text on PTO-bank carve-out and 25-employee threshold confirmed via Justia statute mirror and HB 752 text; no private right of action under the Act confirmed by section (d). Verify against the live OCGA at law.ga.gov before any client-pricing decision.

factual_uncertainty: HB 1010 covers state government employees only; expansion via HB 1078 (2022) added public schools and HB 1209 (2024) raised hours from 120 to 240 for full-time state employees per some sources. Page conservatively cites the original 120-hour figure as the durable baseline; verify the 240-hour expansion against the State Accounting Office circular before deeper claims.

factual_uncertainty: state-PFL table — Maine, Maryland, Delaware, Minnesota, and Rhode Island programmes have rolling 2026 launch dates and benefit-cap revisions; weeks shown reflect each state's published cap, but each state's launch month is subject to administrative timeline shifts. Confirm via that state's administering agency before relying on benefit-availability dates.

factual_uncertainty: voluntary-benefit benchmarks (1.5-2.5% of payroll, 6-8 weeks median paid maternity, 8-12 weeks competitive) drawn from SHRM 2025 and Mercer Southeast regional data; precise regional cuts may differ from national medians. Treat as directional, not specific to any one Georgia subregion.

link_gate_warn: Georgia overview and United States overview links rendered as plain text per link gate (target ships wave 3-4); other sibling links live including Georgia state-tax (live) and Alabama / California paid-family-and-sick-leave (live)

secondary_cta_rule_check: masthead secondary CTA = Employer Cost Calculator (canonical real URL); tagline-close secondary CTA = Crossover Calculator (canonical real URL); both per canonical CTA URL rule
linter_retry_notes: Round 1 caught forbidden word 'foster' in body prose (HB 1010 qualifying-events list, twice). Replaced with 'state-supervised placement of a child' in both the answer-first prose and the table row. Round 2 clean 26/26.

Teamed Client Operations anonymised pullquote (no named SME, no <cite> per pullquote provenance rule)

v2 stake-led capsule applied: stake (no mandate) → size (1.5-2.5% / six-figure loss) → ack (lightness is the opportunity) → promise (federal floor + Georgia carve-outs + HB 1010 context + voluntary benchmarks)

Three named Georgia scenarios used consistently across sections per Steen's batch rule: Mason (Atlanta 30-person fintech), Lily (Savannah 80-person sales team), Carter (Augusta multi-state ops with CA HQ)
Statute references removed from body prose; all section/§ refs live only in Source columns of tables per v2 standard

v2_checks.one_metaphor_max_one_use: terrain/'mine field' implicit in 'narrow carve-outs' phrasing; no mixed metaphors

verify_prompt_facts_audit.prompt_provisional_fact_1: Georgia Family Care Act allows employees to use existing sick leave for family members (does NOT create new leave)
verify_prompt_facts_audit.verification_1: Confirmed via OCGA § 34-1-10 text (Justia mirror) and Georgia HB 752 (2017) statute history. The Act re-allocates existing leave; section (d) confirms no private right of action. Cache layered into table with Source column.
verify_prompt_facts_audit.prompt_provisional_fact_2: HB 1010 (2020) — paid leave for state government employees only (not private sector)
verify_prompt_facts_audit.verification_2: Confirmed via legis.ga.gov HB 1010 text and Georgia DOAS Paid Parental Leave Procedures circular. 120 hours / 3 weeks within 6 months of qualifying event; eligible after 6 months of continuous state service. HB 1078 (2022) extended to public schools. Page conservatively states 120 hours; some sources cite a later HB 1209 (2024) expansion to 240 hours — flagged as factual uncertainty for verification before client-pricing.
verify_prompt_facts_audit.prompt_provisional_fact_3: Georgia is a 'no statute' state — federal FMLA + voluntary benefits are the only employee leave entitlement
verify_prompt_facts_audit.verification_3: Confirmed via cross-reference of A Better Balance state PSL tracker (Georgia not listed), Bett & Associates 2026 PFL tracker (Georgia not listed), and primary OCGA scan (no state PFL or private-sector PSL statute exists). Page accurately frames the no-private-statute distinction while preserving the HB 1010 state-employee and Family Care Act carve-outs.

pullquote_provenance.speaker: Teamed Client Operations
pullquote_provenance.provenance: Anonymised per Steen's pullquote provenance rule. No named individual; no <cite> tag; no MI extraction_id required. Quote articulates the recurring Georgia-leave framing Teamed Client Operations uses with multi-state employers entering Georgia.
